teaching_point: Displacement describes the change in position of an object.
prompt: A toy car starts at point A, moves in a straight line to point B, then returns to point A following a wide parabolic arc. At the end of the motion, the straight-line path from point A to point B is softly highlighted on the table surface. The background is a clean wooden table in a brightly lit room.

criteria.1: Depict both point A and point B clearly and unambiguously in all frames.
criteria.2: Show the toy car moving from point A to point B along a straight-line path only.
criteria.3: Show the toy car returning from point B to point A along a visibly wide, continuous parabolic path only.
criteria.4: Display the car's motion on a flat, clearly visible table surface throughout.
criteria.5: Present the highlight of the straight-line path between A and B only after the car has come to rest at point A.

criteria.1: Display the straight-line highlight only after the toy car has fully stopped at point A.
criteria.2: Omit any straight-line highlight or related visual cue during the car's motion.
criteria.3: Sequence events as: straight-line motion from A to B, parabolic return from B to A, then highlight appears.
criteria.4: Prevent any ghost lines, partial highlights, or premature cues of the straight-line path before the car stops.

criteria.1: Show the toy car moving from A to B in a perfectly straight line without deviation or curvature.
criteria.2: Depict the return path from B to A as a single, smooth, continuous parabolic arc with no abrupt changes in direction or speed.
criteria.3: Ensure the car comes to a complete stop at both point B and point A before any subsequent action or highlight.
criteria.4: Avoid any physically impossible motion, such as instantaneous direction changes, speed jumps, or hovering.
criteria.5: Present the highlight as appearing only after all car motion has ceased, with no causality violations.

criteria.1: Emphasize the straight-line path between A and B as the sole representation of displacement after all car motion is complete.
criteria.2: Use a single, clear, and unambiguous visual highlight to indicate displacement, with no overlap or blending with the car's actual path.
criteria.3: Omit any highlight or emphasis on the parabolic return path or any path other than the straight line as displacement.
criteria.4: Time the appearance of the highlight to reinforce that displacement is measured only after the entire motion is finished.

criteria.1: Maintain fixed and consistent positions for points A and B in all frames.
criteria.2: Keep the car's paths and the straight-line highlight spatially aligned with the same reference points (A and B) at all times.
criteria.3: Display an unchanged table surface and background, free from visual artifacts, throughout the video.
criteria.4: Prevent any shifting or misalignment of the car, highlight, or reference points between scenes.

criteria.1: Omit any highlights, arrows, or visual cues that could be interpreted as alternative displacement paths.
criteria.2: Mark the start and end points of the car's motion clearly and unambiguously at all times.
criteria.3: Exclude extraneous effects such as glowing trails or multiple highlights that could distract from the intended teaching point.
criteria.4: Prevent any visual overlap or blending between the parabolic path and the straight-line highlight that could confuse their meanings.
